textByHashes.ab24594655e98e916dbe018b1e838c0c17c82e39: 'Allo. I don't think you'll be able to visit the mansion today, it's been locked up tight for a while now.
textByHashes.251e3021b1ff26c70736545d4336fb13a59729df: 'Allo, what can I do for you?
textByHashes.f7fd334afbfd64c9df90b52487671a56ccae94d6: I wasn't privy to the reason, but the whole family upped and left not long ago. Since then, I haven't heard a peep from anyone. I was told not to enter the mansion or to speak about their departure.
textByHashes.50d4ccff86c9a34d52bee0cbd6ec59388d40ffee: Didn't say I was going to obey them, did I?
textByHashes.1c068d83b250f254428ed645a3fa6eb7d033d0ed: Why do you want to know about her?
textByHashes.2073ba1c2500e3f6228bd86154d15d685d2eb11e: Good luck, mate. She's a right pain in the...
textByHashes.25d267d84559653fc5b3d1da1de50a805a2fb048: Sorry, you still here? I started thinking about turnips.
textByHashes.43dc2e42cc41237d35c66a5e43f3c563dc752cf0: Why in Gielinor would you want to do that? That's a lost cause if I ever heard one.
textByHashes.79873b793e557bc6c8553ad0d915f8ba85f7a5d4: Not all that much. I worked out here 'til late. I was getting ready to come in for dinner when all the commotion started.
textByHashes.ed1b4d1172afa965f576a54350853e17dc2dcbc1: She must have been in the house, since I didn't see her out here.
textByHashes.fbd56531f25d0ed4cc52d1438421ca5d27ccb112: No, I was on this side of the garden and you can't hear much but the dog from over here.
textByHashes.4de974bb44fc78fa3339db98ea07e5f95ab030e7: Hmm, not that I can remember. It was kept on display as some sort of replica of a great weapon. I'm not much good at history so I can't remember any details.
textByHashes.5e697053c8c8ec930ae03ba95f5bc2a6a656da5d: I think the maid may have dusted it, but I don't really know, I work outside.
textByHashes.5d41326ba46011b6b1cae97534307e038f8019fb: I'm not sure; it could be Anna's or David's.
textByHashes.31de1a3320399b4731a41645cc3c297a89796b32: This salesman came around selling this poison that could fix anything. I think most everyone bought some to use for chores around the mansion.
textByHashes.1a41aa80cf43764b976fba7712e9d2d32ac16840: No, but I requested one of the Sinclairs buy some to use on the compost. I can't remember if they did or not.
textByHashes.b069eec960b632538412767e73314acbf4132e06: Yes, she did. I definitely remember seeing her buying some. I'm pretty sure she didn't use it for her chore either.
textByHashes.2460763fc21d34085268fc4d20702cd90bcf14c4: Have you no shame? We are all grieving at the moment.
textByHashes.e2ba488df1346c9845c43ce1df3e8fd0330c596e: It was Anna. She is seriously unbalanced. She trashed the garden once then tried to blame it on me! I bet it was her. It's just the kind of thing she'd do!
textByHashes.b2a26401b9e7878fb30232986996dabd276edcfa: She really hates me and was arguing with Lord Sinclair about trashing the garden a few days ago.
textByHashes.67536f14daaac6e592d910ff1959911111500ab4: Right here, by my little shed. It's very cosy to sit and think in.
textByHashes.747b1dc2a9ec18653dc8d86dfe3035531b40328b: Not to the best of my recollection.
textByHashes.d557a3b2d553d2a631ff1865f86606acb086af1a: Well, Bob mentioned to me the other day he wanted to get rid of the bees in that hive over there. I think I saw him buying poison
textByHashes.70d40a958131dbdb65530382e2d95609ff3e225d: from that poison salesman the other day. I assume it was to sort out those bees. You'd really have to ask him though.
textByHashes.18959360704439440518ef5628185039ce76b4f6: Thank you for all your help in solving the murder.
